heading: Limitations
body:
The following limitations apply to downgrading your cluster's version:

- You can only downgrade your cluster using the procedure on this page if you
  pinned the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) before upgrading.

- You can only downgrade your cluster to the major version that matches the pinned FCV (Feature Compatibility Version).

- You can only perform this action if the pinned FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) is only one major version below
  the current FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) and you don't have [rapid release](manual/reference/versioning) enabled.

- You can't downgrade to a version after it reaches end-of-life. Ensure pinning occurs
  before the current version reaches end-of-life.

- FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pinning is only available on Dedicated clusters.

- FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) pinning can't be extended beyond 4 weeks from the pin date.

If a downgrade is necessary, and you did not pin the FCV (Feature Compatibility Version) before upgrading, you can:

- Restore to a specific point in time:

  - If you have [Continuous Cloud Backup](pit-restore) enabled for your cluster, you can perform a PIT (Point in Time) restore to restore your cluster to a previous state with
    the older MongoDB version:

    - Create a new cluster with the previous major version.

    - Perform a PIT (Point in Time) restore using the instructions in
      [Restore from Continuous Cloud Backup](restore-from-continuous).

- Restore from an Atlas snapshot:

  - If you have [Cloud Backup](backup-cloud-provider) enabled
    without continuous cloud backups, consider restoring the latest snapshot of
    the cluster based on the previous version to a new cluster:

    - Create a new cluster with the previous major version.

    - Restore the latest snapshot before the MongoDB version upgrade to
      the new cluster using the instructions in [Restore from a Scheduled or On-Demand Snapshot](restore-from-snapshot).
